evidence.kernel_name_note: cuDNN selects 'Kernel2' for FP32 LSTM on H100 — this is the legacy SIMT GEMM path, NOT the WGMMA Tensor Core path (sm90_xmma_gemm_bf16bf16_*). The switch to the Tensor Core path is triggered by BF16/FP16 dtype.
evidence.tensor_core_inference: IDLE (inferred) — FP32 dtype → cuDNN selects non-HMMA Kernel2; BF16 would route to sm90_xmma_gemm_bf16bf16_* (WGMMA, 4x throughput on H100)
evidence.metric_availability_note: cuDNN operators are opaque to ncu — TC%, occupancy, SM%, DRAM% all unavailable. Bottleneck inferred from (1) kernel name 'Kernel2' = cuDNN non-HMMA path, (2) model dtype FP32, (3) H100 Tensor Core dispatch rule.

evidence.kernel_name_note: tilesize32x32x8 = minimal tile — chosen because output is only [32, 10]. Tiny GEMM, wave-starved by construction. At 0.7% of total time, not a meaningful optimization target.
Temporal reduction over [32, 128, 512] — memory-bound by nature. At 0.4% of total time, not a meaningful optimization target.

edge_case_notes.ncu_replay_timing: All durations are from ncu kernel-replay mode (2-5x longer than real wall-clock). Use only for relative comparison between baseline and optimized profiles.
edge_case_notes.cudnn_metric_unavailable: aten::_cudnn_rnn dispatches to cuDNN's internal LSTM implementation. Standard hardware counters (tensor_core_active_pct, achieved_occupancy, sm_throughput_pct, dram_throughput_pct) are all null for cuDNN operators. Bottleneck classification uses kernel name + dtype inference instead of measured counters. This is expected behavior for cuDNN operators — the profiler correctly attributes timing to the operator but cannot measure cuDNN's internal kernel metrics.

architecture_notes: H100 PCIe: 114 SMs (device_name null in profile — using H100 defaults). Ridge point ~200 FLOP/byte for BF16. WGMMA (Tensor Core) path requires BF16/FP16 inputs for cuDNN LSTM. FP32 LSTM uses legacy SIMT Kernel2 path, which cannot use Tensor Cores.
optimization_ceiling: aten::_cudnn_rnn accounts for 98.9% of attributed time. BF16 dtype promotion is the single actionable optimization: routes cuDNN from SIMT Kernel2 to WGMMA sm90_xmma_gemm_bf16bf16_* (expected 2-4x faster on H100 for this GEMM size). The remaining 1.1% (addmm + mean + copy_ + fill_) is below meaningful optimization threshold.
